name: Granadella - Bellaguarda - Bovera - Granadella — Circular through Les Garrigues
name_original: Granadella - Bellaguarda - Bóvera - Granadella — Circular por Les Garrigues
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Bellaguarda traverse the rural landscape of Lleida, Catalonia, characterized by rolling hills and agricultural areas. The region features a mix of open plains and more elevated terrain, particularly towards the Montsant Mountains. Cyclists can expect routes that wind through olive groves and vineyards, offering varied gradients. The area provides a tranquil setting for cycling, with routes connecting small villages and offering views of the surrounding countryside. Best touring cycling routes around Bellaguarda The most popular touring cycling route is Granadella - Bellaguarda - Bovera - Granadella — Circular through Les Garrigues, a 15.8 miles (25.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 54 minutes to complete. This route offers a moderate challenge through the Les Garrigues region. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Santa Llúcia Hospital Cave – View of the Montsant Mountains loop from la Palma d'Ebre, a difficult 58.3 miles (93.8 km) path. This route provides extensive views of the Montsant Mountains and covers significant elevation gain. Local touring cyclists also love the Plaça Major de La Granadella – Santa Llúcia Hospital Cave loop from La Granadella, a 36.6 miles (58.8 km) trail leading through the rural landscape of La Granadella, often completed in about 3 hours 56 minutes. Touring cycling around Bellaguarda is defined by rolling hills, agricultural plains, and distant mountain views. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Bellaguarda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 190 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Bellaguarda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Bellaguarda?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes around Bellaguarda. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews, and have been explored by more than 190 touring cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Bellaguarda?
A: The terrain around Bellaguarda features a rural landscape of Lleida, Catalonia, characterized by rolling hills and agricultural areas. You'll find a mix of open plains and more elevated sections, especially towards the Montsant Mountains. Routes often wind through olive groves and vineyards, offering varied gradients suitable for touring cyclists.

Q: Are there options for different skill levels among the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the routes in Bellaguarda offer options for various ability levels. While there are no easy routes listed, you can find 4 moderate routes and 12 difficult routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962266923/granadella-bellaguarda-bovera-granadella-circular-through-les-garrigues" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granadella - Bellaguarda - Bovera - Granadella — Circular through Les Garrigues&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38197083" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Llúcia Hospital Cave – View of the Montsant Mountains loop from la Palma d'Ebre&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult path with significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Bellaguarda?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Bellaguarda are circular. Another well-liked circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31502070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaça Major de La Granadella – Santa Llúcia Hospital Cave loop from La Granadella&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features to see along the cycling routes?
A: Along the touring cycling routes, you can explore various points of interest. Natural features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/843527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cadolles Fondes Deep Pools&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/591296" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Llibreria Crevice&lt;/a&gt;. For historical and cultural sights, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/305718" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escaladei Charterhouse&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/843525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Sant Bartomeu de Fraguerau&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also offer stunning views, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4584288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Priorat Balcony&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/845341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Fraguerau gorge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the average duration and distance of touring cycling routes in Bellaguarda?
A: The touring cycling routes around Bellaguarda vary significantly in duration and distance. Moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962266923/granadella-bellaguarda-bovera-granadella-circular-through-les-garrigues" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granadella - Bellaguarda - Bovera - Granadella — Circular through Les Garrigues&lt;/a&gt; can take around 1 hour 54 minutes to complete over 15.8 miles (25.4 km). More challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38197083" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Llúcia Hospital Cave – View of the Montsant Mountains loop from la Palma d'Ebre&lt;/a&gt;, can extend to 58.3 miles (93.8 km) and take over 6 hours.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, Bellaguarda offers several challenging long-distance routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38197083" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Llúcia Hospital Cave – View of the Montsant Mountains loop from la Palma d'Ebre&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 58.3 miles (93.8 km) path that provides extensive views of the Montsant Mountains and covers significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31488684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaça Major de La Granadella loop from La Granadella&lt;/a&gt;, which is 42.5 miles (68.4 km) long.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route near Bellaguarda?
A: While specific parking locations are not detailed for each route, Bellaguarda and the surrounding small villages like La Granadella, which serve as common starting points for these routes, typically offer local parking facilities. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: Is public transport available to access touring cycling routes in Bellaguarda?
A: Bellaguarda is a rural area in Lleida, Catalonia. Public transport options might be limited compared to larger urban centers. It's recommended to check local bus schedules or regional train services that connect to nearby towns, as direct access to all route starting points via public transport may not be available.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Bellaguarda?
A: The touring cycling routes in Bellaguarda are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an impressive average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the tranquil setting, the varied gradients through olive groves and vineyards, and the distant views of the Montsant Mountains, making for a rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Bellaguarda?
A: While the guide lists 4 moderate routes and 12 difficult routes, specific 'family-friendly' designations are not provided. However, the moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962266923/granadella-bellaguarda-bovera-granadella-circular-through-les-garrigues" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granadella - Bellaguarda - Bovera - Granadella — Circular through Les Garrigues&lt;/a&gt;, which is 15.8 miles (25.4 km) long, might be suitable for families with older children or those accustomed to cycling on rolling terrain.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Bellaguarda?
A: The rural landscape of Bellaguarda generally welcomes dogs, especially on outdoor trails. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock. Be sure to carry water for your pet, as specific dog-friendly amenities might be scarce along the routes.
tips: Parish church of the town of Bovera, in the Garrigues region. At present, it resembles a baroque temple, but belongs to the transitional Romanesque. We cannot pass by the church without taking a walk through the quiet and welcoming town of Bovera.

From the top of the town of Granadella, we will find this viewpoint that, as the name says, is facing the Tierra del Ebro. We will have a beautiful picture with views of Montsant, the Sierra de Cavalls, the Sierra de la Fatarella, the nuclear power plant of Ascó and the mountains of Tierras del Ebro.
